card.intuition: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Pre-train a transformer encoder on two tasks: Masked Language Modelling (MLM) — randomly mask 15% of tokens, predict them using bidirectional context; Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) — classify whether sentence B follows sentence A. Fine-tune the [CLS] representation or token representations for downstream tasks.
card.analogy: A student who reads entire chapters with random words blanked out (MLM) — forced to understand context from both sides to fill the gap. After enough practice, they understand language deeply and can quickly learn new tasks (fine-tuning) like answering questions or classifying sentiment.
card.time_complexity: O(T² · d) per forward pass — transformer encoder
card.space_complexity: O(V · d) for token embeddings + O(L · d²) for L layers
when_to_use: Token-level tasks: NER, POS tagging, extractive QA
Sentence-level tasks: sentiment, textual entailment, sentence similarity
Foundation model: fine-tune on <1000 examples for strong performance
gotchas: MLM masking strategy: 80% [MASK], 10% random token, 10% unchanged — prevents model relying solely on [MASK] signal
Max sequence length 512 — use Longformer or chunking for longer docs
NSP was found less useful in RoBERTa — removing it and training longer with more data often works better

exercise.prompt: Implement the MLM token masking strategy.
exercise.starter_code:
import random

MASK_ID  = 103
CLS_ID   = 101
SEP_ID   = 102

def mlm_mask(token_ids, vocab_size, mask_prob=0.15, seed=None):
    """
    Apply BERT MLM masking to token_ids.
    Returns (masked_ids, labels) where labels[i] = original id if masked else -100.
    Strategy for each selected token:
      80% -> MASK_ID
      10% -> random token
      10% -> unchanged
    Special tokens ([CLS],[SEP]) are never masked.
    """
    if seed is not None: random.seed(seed)
    masked = list(token_ids)
    labels = [-100] * len(token_ids)
    for i, tid in enumerate(token_ids):
        if tid in (CLS_ID, SEP_ID): continue
        if random.random() < mask_prob:
            labels[i] = tid
            r = random.random()
            if r < 0.8:
                masked[i] = ___________
            elif r < 0.9:
                masked[i] = random.randint(0, vocab_size - 1)
            # else: keep original
    return masked, labels
hints: 80% of selected tokens are replaced with the special [MASK] token (id=103).
exercise.test_code:
random.seed(42)
ids = [101, 7592, 2003, 1037, 3231, 102]  # [CLS] hello is a test [SEP]
masked, labels = mlm_mask(ids, vocab_size=30522, seed=42)

# CLS and SEP should never be masked
assert masked[0] == 101 and labels[0] == -100
assert masked[-1] == 102 and labels[-1] == -100

# Masked positions have non -100 labels
masked_positions = [i for i in range(len(labels)) if labels[i] != -100]
assert all(labels[i] == ids[i] for i in masked_positions)

# Masked tokens are MASK_ID, random, or unchanged
for i in masked_positions:
    assert masked[i] in [MASK_ID] + list(range(30522)) or masked[i] == ids[i]
print('PASS')
